title: Platform Publishing Integration - Hazina ISocialPublisher Pipeline for 4 Major Platforms
description:
## SUMMARY (Expert Panel Consensus)
Real API publishing using Hazina's existing ISocialPublisher implementations for LinkedIn, Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram.
OAuth token management via SocialMediaPublishingService with refresh/retry logic already in client-manager.
Requires platform API verification (todo tasks) for production keys - can develop with sandbox/test keys first.
Backend: wire existing Hazina publishers into SocialMediaPostController. Frontend: extend MultiPlatformPostCreator.
Expert panel: API integration engineers, OAuth security specialists, social media API experts, rate limiting architects.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Phase A: Hazina Publisher Wiring (Day 1-2)
1. **Existing Infrastructure** (already built):
   - `ISocialPublisher` interface with `PublishPostAsync()`, `DeletePostAsync()`, `GetPostMetricsAsync()`
   - Publishers: `LinkedInPublisher`, `TwitterPublisher`, `FacebookPublisher` (in Hazina)
   - `SocialMediaPublishingService` in client-manager already handles token management
   - `MultiPlatformPublishingService` orchestrates multi-platform publishing
2. **Wire up**:
   - Register Hazina publishers in DI container (Program.cs)
   - Map `SocialMediaPost` entity to publisher input format
   - Store external post IDs in `PostLink` entity after successful publish
   - Implement error handling with retry logic (exponential backoff)

### Phase B: OAuth Token Lifecycle (Day 2-3)
1. Token refresh before publish attempt (check expiry)
2. Handle revoked/expired tokens gracefully (notify user)
3. Rate limit tracking per platform per account
4. Store API rate limit headers and respect cooldown periods

### Phase C: Platform-Specific Adaptations (Day 3-4)
1. **LinkedIn**: Text post + image upload, company page vs personal
2. **Facebook**: Page post via Graph API, image/video support
3. **Twitter/X**: 280 char limit, thread support for long content
4. **Instagram**: Image required, hashtag limit (30), no link in post
5. Use existing `ContentAdaptationService` for platform-specific formatting

### Phase D: Error Handling & Monitoring (Day 4)
1. Retry queue for failed publishes (Hangfire job)
2. Status tracking: PENDING -> PUBLISHING -> PUBLISHED / FAILED
3. Error notifications via SignalR to frontend
4. Log all API interactions for debugging

### Technical References
- **Publishers**: `Hazina.Tools.Services.Social\Publishers\LinkedInPublisher.cs` etc.
- **Publishing Service**: `ClientManagerAPI\Services\SocialMediaPublishingService.cs`
- **Multi-Platform**: `ClientManagerAPI\Services\MultiPlatformPublishingService.cs`
- **Post Controller**: `ClientManagerAPI\Controllers\SocialMediaPostController.cs`
- **Content Adaptation**: `ClientManagerAPI\Services\ContentAdaptationService.cs`
- **Token Management**: OAuth middleware in Program.cs

### Unblocked By
- API verification tasks (869c2e17j, 869c2e17m, 869c2e17t) for production keys
- Can develop with sandbox/test keys before verification completes

### Definition of Done
- [ ] LinkedIn publishing functional (text + image)
- [ ] Facebook page publishing functional
- [ ] Twitter posting with thread support
- [ ] Instagram image posting
- [ ] OAuth token refresh before publish
- [ ] Rate limiting respected
- [ ] Failed publish retry via Hangfire
- [ ] External post IDs stored
- [ ] Error notifications to frontend

title: Analytics Dashboard - Real-Time Engagement Metrics with Platform API Data & AI Insights
description:
## SUMMARY (Expert Panel Consensus)
Engagement analytics dashboard using Hazina ISocialProvider.FetchEngagementAsync() for real platform data.
Extends existing SocialMediaAnalyticsController + ROICalculatorController infrastructure in client-manager.
Frontend: recharts-based dashboard extending ROIDashboard.tsx and SmartScheduleDashboard.tsx patterns.
AI insights via Hazina LLM for trend analysis and posting recommendations. Estimated 5-6 days development.
Expert panel: Analytics engineers, data visualization experts, social media strategists, AI/ML recommendation specialists.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Phase A: Data Collection Pipeline (Day 1-2)
1. Use Hazina `ISocialProvider.FetchEngagementAsync()` per connected account
2. Schedule Hangfire job: `SyncEngagementMetricsJob` - runs every 6 hours
3. Store metrics in new `EngagementMetric` entity:
   - PostId, Platform, Date, Likes, Shares, Comments, Views, Reach, Impressions
4. Aggregate daily/weekly/monthly via `SocialMediaMetricsService`

### Phase B: Dashboard API (Day 2-3)
1. Extend `SocialMediaAnalyticsController`:
   - `GET /api/analytics/overview` - KPI cards (total reach, engagement rate, growth)
   - `GET /api/analytics/platform-comparison` - Cross-platform chart data
   - `GET /api/analytics/best-times` - Optimal posting times (extend `SmartSchedulingService`)
   - `GET /api/analytics/content-performance` - By content type
   - `GET /api/analytics/hashtag-performance` - Hashtag effectiveness
   - `GET /api/analytics/trends` - Time-series engagement data
   - `POST /api/analytics/export` - Export to CSV/PDF

### Phase C: React Dashboard UI (Day 3-5)
1. Create `AnalyticsDashboard.tsx` extending patterns from `ROIDashboard.tsx`:
   - KPI cards row (total reach, avg engagement, follower growth, top post)
   - Platform comparison bar chart (recharts)
   - Engagement trend line chart with date range selector
   - Content type donut chart
   - Best posting times heatmap
   - Top performing posts table
2. Custom date range picker component
3. Platform filter toggles
4. Export button (CSV/PDF via existing `PdfGenerator`)

### Phase D: AI Insights (Day 5-6)
1. Weekly AI analysis via Hazina LLM:
   - Trend detection (what's working, what's declining)
   - Content recommendations (post type, timing, hashtags)
   - Competitor benchmark suggestions
2. Display as "AI Insights" card in dashboard
3. Store insights for historical comparison

### Technical References
- **Analytics Controller**: `ClientManagerAPI\Controllers\SocialMediaAnalyticsController.cs`
- **Metrics Service**: `ClientManagerAPI\Services\SocialMediaMetricsService.cs`
- **ROI Dashboard**: `ClientManagerFrontend\src\components\pages\ROIDashboard.tsx`
- **Smart Scheduling**: `ClientManagerAPI\Services\SmartSchedulingService.cs`
- **Social Provider**: `Hazina.Tools.Services.Social\Abstractions\ISocialProvider.cs`
- **Charts**: recharts (already in dependencies)
- **PDF Export**: `ClientManagerAPI\Services\PdfGenerator.cs`

### Unblocked By
- Platform Publishing Integration (869c1dnww) - needs published posts to track
- API verification tasks for engagement data access

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Engagement metrics synced from all connected platforms
- [ ] Dashboard with 6+ chart types
- [ ] Custom date range filtering
- [ ] Platform comparison view
- [ ] Best posting times analysis
- [ ] CSV/PDF export
- [ ] AI insights card with recommendations
- [ ] Hangfire sync job running every 6h

title: Media Library - Drag-Drop Upload, Platform Cropping & Stock Photo Integration
description:
## SUMMARY (Expert Panel Consensus)
Centralized media library extending existing ChatImageController + ImageSetGenerationService with platform-specific cropping.
Drag-drop upload using existing document upload hooks, media search/filter via embeddings similarity.
Stock photo integration (Unsplash/Pexels) using Hazina web scraping or direct API. CDN-ready asset pipeline.
Frontend: new MediaLibrary component with grid/list views using existing shadcn/Radix patterns.
Expert panel: Media processing engineers, CDN architects, UX designers, image optimization specialists, accessibility experts.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Phase A: Media Entity & Storage (Day 1-2)
1. Create `MediaAsset` entity extending EF Core:
   - Id, FileName, MimeType, FileSize, Width, Height
   - AltText (from ImageAltTextService), Tags[], UploadedBy, UploadedAt
   - Variants: JSON array of {size, path, width, height}
   - EmbeddingVector: for semantic search
2. Create `MediaController`:
   - `POST /api/media/upload` - Multi-file drag-drop upload
   - `GET /api/media` - List with search/filter/pagination
   - `GET /api/media/{id}` - Detail with variants
   - `POST /api/media/{id}/crop` - Platform-specific crop
   - `DELETE /api/media/{id}` - Soft delete
3. Store originals in `C:\stores\brand2boost\{projectId}\media\`

### Phase B: Image Processing Pipeline (Day 2-3)
1. On upload, auto-generate:
   - Thumbnail (200x200)
   - Medium (800px wide)
   - Platform variants:
     - LinkedIn: 1200x627
     - Instagram: 1080x1080 (square), 1080x1350 (portrait)
     - Twitter: 1200x675
     - Facebook: 1200x630
2. Use Hazina `IImageTool.EditAsync()` for cropping/resizing
3. Auto-generate alt text via existing `ImageAltTextService`
4. Generate embeddings for semantic search via `EmbeddingsService`

### Phase C: React Media Library UI (Day 3-5)
1. Create `MediaLibrary.tsx`:
   - Drag-drop zone (extending `useDocumentUpload` hook)
   - Grid/list toggle view
   - Search (by name, tag, or semantic via embeddings)
   - Filter by type (image/video/document), date, platform
   - Bulk select + bulk delete
   - Image preview with zoom
2. Create `PlatformCropModal.tsx`:
   - Visual crop tool with platform preset ratios
   - Preview per platform
   - Save cropped variant
3. Integrate into post creation flow (MultiPlatformPostCreator)

### Phase D: Stock Photo Integration (Day 5-6)
1. Unsplash API integration: search, preview, download
2. Pexels API integration: search, preview, download
3. Create `StockPhotoSearch.tsx` component
4. Auto-attribute photographer credit
5. Downloaded stock photos go into media library as regular assets

### Technical References
- **Image Controller**: `ClientManagerAPI\Controllers\ChatImageController.cs`
- **Alt Text Service**: `ClientManagerAPI\Services\ImageAltTextService.cs`
- **Image Generation**: `ClientManagerAPI\Services\ImageSetGenerationService.cs`
- **Upload Hook**: `ClientManagerFrontend\src\hooks\useDocumentUpload.ts`
- **Image Tool**: `Hazina.Tools.Services.Images\Abstractions\IImageTool.cs`
- **Embeddings**: `ClientManagerAPI\Services\EmbeddingsService.cs`

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Drag-drop multi-file upload
- [ ] Auto-generated platform variants (4 platforms)
- [ ] Alt text auto-generation
- [ ] Media library with search/filter/grid
- [ ] Platform crop tool
- [ ] Semantic search via embeddings
- [ ] Unsplash + Pexels stock photo search
- [ ] Integration in post creation flow

title: Reddit OAuth Integration - Connect Account, Import Content & Publish via Hazina Provider
description:
## SUMMARY (Expert Panel Consensus)
Reddit OAuth integration using existing Hazina RedditProvider pattern + client-manager OAuth callback infrastructure.
Detailed step-by-step already documented: Reddit app creation, backend secrets, frontend config, OAuth flow.
Extends existing social connection flow (same as LinkedIn/Facebook) with Reddit-specific scopes.
Import pipeline via ISocialProvider.ImportContentAsync() for posts/comments. Estimated 2-3 days.
Expert panel: OAuth security engineers, Reddit API specialists, social media integration architects.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Phase A: Reddit App & Configuration (Day 1)
1. Create Reddit web app at reddit.com/prefs/apps
2. Configure redirect URIs: `https://localhost:5173/social-callback` + `https://app.brand2boost.com/social-callback`
3. Add secrets to `appsettings.Secrets.json`: RedditOAuth.ClientId, ClientSecret, UserAgent
4. Add REDDIT_CLIENT_ID to `ClientManagerFrontend/public/config.js`

### Phase B: Backend Integration (Day 1-2)
1. Wire Hazina `RedditProvider` into DI container
2. Add Reddit OAuth callback handler in `AuthController` (follow LinkedIn pattern)
3. Token exchange endpoint: authorization code -> access + refresh token
4. Store connected account via existing `ConnectedAccount` infrastructure
5. Scopes: identity, read, history (optional: mysubreddits)

### Phase C: Frontend OAuth Flow (Day 2)
1. Add Reddit to `PlatformConnectionHub.tsx` / `PlatformConnectionGrid.tsx`
2. Reddit card with icon + connect button
3. OAuth popup flow (same as LinkedIn): popup -> authorize -> callback -> close
4. Connected account visible in UI with disconnect option

### Phase D: Content Import (Day 2-3)
1. Use `ISocialProvider.ImportContentAsync()` for Reddit posts
2. Map Reddit posts to `SocialMediaPost` entity format
3. Import comments and karma data
4. Display in Imported Content section

### Technical References
- **Reddit Provider**: `Hazina.Tools.Services.Social\Providers\RedditProvider.cs`
- **OAuth Pattern**: `ClientManagerFrontend\src\components\containers\LinkedInCallback.tsx`
- **Platform Hub**: `ClientManagerFrontend\src\components\social\PlatformConnectionHub.tsx`
- **Auth Controller**: `ClientManagerAPI\Controllers\AuthController.cs`
- **Social Import**: `ClientManagerAPI\Controllers\SocialImportController.cs`

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Reddit app created with correct redirect URIs
- [ ] Backend secrets configured
- [ ] OAuth popup flow working
- [ ] Account stored in Connected Accounts
- [ ] Content import functional
- [ ] Disconnect account working
- [ ] Production redirect URI configured

title: Platform Publisher with AI Image Matching - Smart Visual Content Selection & Generation
description:
## SUMMARY (Expert Panel Consensus)
Intelligent image matching using Hazina embedding similarity (cosine distance) between post content and media library.
Publishing flow: select draft -> pick platform -> AI generates platform-specific content -> match/generate image -> publish.
Extends MultiPlatformPostCreator with image fit scoring. Uses existing EmbeddingsService for semantic matching.
AI image generation via ImageSetGenerationService for new visuals. Estimated 4-5 days development.
Expert panel: Recommendation system engineers, image search specialists, UX designers, AI image generation experts.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Phase A: Image Matching Service (Day 1-2)
1. Create `ImageMatchingService`:
   - Input: post text content
   - Generate embedding for post text via `EmbeddingsService`
   - Compare against all media library image embeddings
   - Calculate cosine similarity = fit percentage (0-100%)
   - Return ranked list (top 5) with fit scores
2. API endpoint: `POST /api/social-posts/{id}/match-images`
3. Also match against generated images from chat history

### Phase B: Publishing Platform Selector UI (Day 2-3)
1. Create `PlatformPublisher.tsx`:
   - Grid of all supported platforms
   - CONNECTED: full color, clickable to publish
   - DISCONNECTED: gray, click to connect (redirect to settings)
   - Badge showing connected account count per platform
2. Create `PublishConfirmDialog.tsx`:
   - Platform-specific preview of post
   - Best matching image with fit percentage bar
   - "Generate New Image" button
   - Character count validation
   - Publish / Schedule buttons

### Phase C: Image Selection Panel (Day 3-4)
1. Create `ImageMatchingPanel.tsx`:
   - Top 5 matched images with fit percentage badges
   - Click to select different image
   - "Generate with AI" button -> opens AI prompt dialog
   - Preview selected image in platform frame
2. AI generation: extend `ImageSetGenerationService` to auto-generate prompt from post content

### Phase D: Publish Execution (Day 4-5)
1. Wire `ISocialPublisher.PublishPostAsync()` with selected image
2. Platform-specific image formatting (resize to platform specs)
3. Progress indicator during publish
4. Success/failure notification with link to published post
5. Store external post ID in `PostLink` entity

### Technical References
- **Embeddings**: `ClientManagerAPI\Services\EmbeddingsService.cs`
- **Image Generation**: `ClientManagerAPI\Services\ImageSetGenerationService.cs`
- **Multi-Platform Creator**: `ClientManagerFrontend\src\components\social\MultiPlatformPostCreator.tsx`
- **Publishers**: `Hazina.Tools.Services.Social\Publishers\`
- **Image Suggestion**: `ClientManagerAPI\Services\ImageSuggestionService.cs`
- **Content Adaptation**: `ClientManagerAPI\Services\ContentAdaptationService.cs`

### Depends On
- Platform Publishing Integration (869c1dnww)
- Media Library (869c1dnx7) for image pool

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Image matching returns top 5 with fit percentages
- [ ] Platform grid with connected/disconnected states
- [ ] Publish confirmation with platform preview
- [ ] Image selection panel with AI generation
- [ ] End-to-end publish flow working
- [ ] External post IDs stored
- [ ] Error handling with retry

title: Integration Testing & Documentation - Connected Accounts Test Suite & API Docs
description:
## SUMMARY (Expert Panel Consensus)
Comprehensive test suite for connected accounts using existing ClientManagerAPI.Tests + IntegrationTests infrastructure.
Covers NoAuth, WordPress AppPassword, OAuth flows, permission enforcement, and backward compatibility.
API documentation update via Swagger/OpenAPI annotations. User guide for account connection flow.
Estimated 2-3 days testing + documentation.
Expert panel: QA engineers, API documentation specialists, security testers, integration test architects.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Phase A: Unit Tests (Day 1)
1. Extend `ClientManagerAPI.Tests`:
   - Test `SocialMediaPublishingService` with mocked publishers
   - Test `ContentAdaptationService` per platform
   - Test OAuth token refresh logic
   - Test rate limiting middleware
   - Test `ImageMatchingService` cosine similarity
2. Mock Hazina publishers for isolated testing

### Phase B: Integration Tests (Day 1-2)
1. Extend `ClientManagerAPI.IntegrationTests`:
   - Full OAuth flow: authorize -> callback -> token stored
   - Publish flow: create post -> select platform -> publish
   - Import flow: connect account -> import content
   - Token refresh: expired token -> auto-refresh -> retry
   - Permission enforcement: unauthorized user -> 403
   - WordPress AppPassword: connect -> test -> publish
2. Use in-memory DB for isolation

### Phase C: API Documentation (Day 2)
1. Add Swagger annotations to all social media endpoints
2. Document OAuth callback flow with sequence diagrams
3. Document rate limits per platform
4. Create Postman collection for manual testing

### Phase D: User Guide (Day 2-3)
1. Step-by-step connection guide per platform
2. Troubleshooting section (common OAuth errors)
3. Screenshots of connection flow
4. FAQ section

### Technical References
- **Test Project**: `ClientManagerAPI.Tests\`
- **Integration Tests**: `ClientManagerAPI.IntegrationTests\`
- **Swagger Config**: Program.cs Swagger setup
- **Auth Controller**: `ClientManagerAPI\Controllers\AuthController.cs`

### Depends On
- Platform Publishing (869c1dnww)
- Phase 5 Publisher (869bz3gzc)

### Definition of Done
- [ ] 20+ unit tests for social media services
- [ ] 10+ integration tests for full flows
- [ ] Swagger docs updated for all endpoints
- [ ] User guide with screenshots
- [ ] Backward compatibility verified
- [ ] All tests green in CI

title: Fix Login Route - /login Path Not Rendering Auth Page Correctly
description:
## SUMMARY (Expert Panel Consensus)
Routing bug where /login path doesn't render the authentication page. Likely React Router configuration issue.
AuthPage.tsx handles both login and registration at /login and /register routes.
Possible causes: route guard redirect loop, missing route definition, or ProtectedRoute interference.
Quick debug via React DevTools + network tab. Estimated 1-2 hours fix.
Expert panel: React Router specialists, authentication flow engineers, frontend debugging experts.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Phase A: Diagnosis (30 min)
1. Check `App.tsx` route definitions for `/login` path
2. Verify `AuthPage.tsx` renders for unauthenticated users
3. Check if `ProtectedRoute` is intercepting `/login` and redirecting
4. Check `authStore.ts` for redirect logic on load
5. Test: clear cookies/localStorage -> navigate to /login

### Phase B: Fix (30 min - 1 hour)
1. Likely fix: ensure `/login` route is OUTSIDE `ProtectedRoute` wrapper
2. Verify route order in React Router (specific before catch-all)
3. Check for redirect loop: login -> check auth -> redirect to login
4. Test both direct navigation (/login) and redirect from protected route

### Phase C: Verification (30 min)
1. Test scenarios:
   - Direct /login navigation (unauthenticated)
   - Redirect to /login from protected route
   - /login when already authenticated (should redirect to dashboard)
   - /register route still works
   - OAuth callback routes unaffected

### Technical References
- **App Router**: `ClientManagerFrontend\src\App.tsx`
- **Auth Page**: `ClientManagerFrontend\src\components\containers\AuthPage.tsx`
- **Protected Route**: `ClientManagerFrontend\src\components\containers\ProtectedRoute.tsx`
- **Auth Store**: `ClientManagerFrontend\src\stores\authStore.ts`

### Definition of Done
- [ ] /login renders AuthPage for unauthenticated users
- [ ] Redirect from protected routes works
- [ ] Already-authenticated users redirect to dashboard
- [ ] /register still functional
- [ ] OAuth callbacks unaffected

title: Facebook/Meta API Verification - Business Verification & App Review Process
description:
## SUMMARY (Expert Panel Consensus)
Meta Business Verification + App Review required for production Facebook/Instagram API access.
Existing Hazina FacebookProvider + InstagramProvider are code-ready - this task is about platform approval.
Requires: Meta Business Suite account, business verification documents, privacy policy URL, app review submission.
Critical path: blocks all Facebook + Instagram publishing in production. Estimated 1-2 weeks approval time.
Expert panel: Meta API specialists, business verification consultants, privacy policy writers, app review strategists.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Phase A: Meta Business Setup (Day 1)
1. Create/verify Meta Business Suite account at business.facebook.com
2. Add Brand2Boost as business entity
3. Upload business verification documents (KvK registration, utility bill, domain verification)
4. Wait for business verification (typically 1-5 business days)

### Phase B: App Configuration (Day 1)
1. Go to developers.facebook.com -> create app (or update existing)
2. App type: Business
3. Add products:
   - Facebook Login (for OAuth)
   - Pages API (for page publishing)
   - Instagram Graph API (for Instagram publishing)
4. Configure OAuth redirect URIs:
   - `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
   - `https://app.brand2boost.com/social-callback`
5. Generate App Secret -> store in `appsettings.Secrets.json`

### Phase C: App Review Submission (Day 2)
1. Required permissions:
   - `pages_show_list` - List user's pages
   - `pages_read_engagement` - Read page engagement
   - `pages_manage_posts` - Create/edit/delete page posts
   - `instagram_basic` - Instagram basic access
   - `instagram_content_publish` - Instagram publishing
2. For each permission:
   - Record screencast demo showing feature usage
   - Write usage description explaining why needed
   - Provide test account credentials
3. Submit for review

### Phase D: Production Deployment (After Approval)
1. Switch from development to live mode
2. Update production secrets with live app credentials
3. Test full OAuth -> publish flow on production
4. Monitor API rate limits and quota

### Technical References
- **Facebook Provider**: `Hazina.Tools.Services.Social\Providers\FacebookProvider.cs`
- **Instagram Provider**: `Hazina.Tools.Services.Social\Providers\InstagramProvider.cs`
- **Facebook Publisher**: `Hazina.Tools.Services.Social\Publishers\FacebookPublisher.cs`
- **OAuth Callback**: `ClientManagerFrontend\src\components\containers\LinkedInCallback.tsx` (pattern)
- **Secrets Config**: `ClientManagerAPI\appsettings.Secrets.json`

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Meta Business verified
- [ ] App created with correct products
- [ ] OAuth redirect URIs configured
- [ ] App review submitted with screencasts
- [ ] App review APPROVED
- [ ] Live mode activated
- [ ] Production credentials deployed

title: LinkedIn API Verification - Company Page Verification & API Products Approval
description:
## SUMMARY (Expert Panel Consensus)
LinkedIn Company Page verification + Marketing Developer Platform access for production publishing.
Existing Hazina LinkedInProvider + LinkedInPublisher are fully implemented and tested locally.
Requires: verified LinkedIn Company Page, API Products request (Share on LinkedIn, Sign In with LinkedIn).
LinkedIn has fastest approval process (~1-3 days). Critical for professional content publishing.
Expert panel: LinkedIn API specialists, B2B marketing strategists, developer platform consultants.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Phase A: Company Page Verification (Day 1)
1. Create LinkedIn Company Page for Brand2Boost (or verify existing)
2. Add company details: logo, description, website URL, industry
3. Verify admin access to company page
4. Ensure at least 1 admin employee profile linked

### Phase B: Developer Application (Day 1)
1. Go to linkedin.com/developers -> create app
2. Associate with verified Company Page
3. Request API Products:
   - **Share on LinkedIn** - for publishing posts
   - **Sign In with LinkedIn using OpenID Connect** - for OAuth
   - **Marketing Developer Platform** (if available) - for analytics
4. Configure OAuth redirect URIs:
   - `https://localhost:5173/social-callback`
   - `https://app.brand2boost.com/social-callback`
5. Generate Client ID + Client Secret

### Phase C: Configuration (Day 1-2)
1. Store in `appsettings.Secrets.json`: LinkedIn.ClientId, ClientSecret
2. Store in `config.js`: LINKEDIN_CLIENT_ID
3. Verify existing OAuth flow works with new credentials
4. Test: connect -> authorize -> callback -> account stored

### Phase D: Production Testing (After Approval)
1. Test full publish flow: create post -> publish to LinkedIn
2. Verify company page posting vs personal posting
3. Test engagement metrics fetching
4. Monitor API rate limits (100 calls/day for Share API)

### Technical References
- **LinkedIn Provider**: `Hazina.Tools.Services.Social\Providers\LinkedInProvider.cs`
- **LinkedIn Publisher**: `Hazina.Tools.Services.Social\Publishers\LinkedInPublisher.cs`
- **OAuth Callback**: `ClientManagerFrontend\src\components\containers\LinkedInCallback.tsx`
- **Secrets Config**: `ClientManagerAPI\appsettings.Secrets.json`

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Company Page verified on LinkedIn
- [ ] Developer app created and associated
- [ ] Share on LinkedIn product approved
- [ ] Sign In product approved
- [ ] OAuth flow tested with production credentials
- [ ] Publishing tested end-to-end

title: Instagram API Verification - Business Account Setup & Meta App Review
description:
## SUMMARY (Expert Panel Consensus)
Instagram API access requires Facebook/Meta App Review (same app as Facebook verification task).
Instagram Graph API requires: Instagram Business Account linked to Facebook Page + Meta app with instagram_content_publish.
Can be combined with Facebook/Meta verification (869c2e17j) as they share the same Meta app.
Existing Hazina InstagramProvider handles OAuth + content management. Estimated: combined with Facebook task.
Expert panel: Instagram API specialists, Meta platform consultants, content publishing engineers.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Phase A: Instagram Business Account (Day 1)
1. Convert personal Instagram to Business Account (or create new)
2. Link Instagram Business Account to Facebook Page
3. Verify connection in Meta Business Suite

### Phase B: Meta App Configuration (Combined with Facebook task)
1. Add Instagram Graph API product to existing Meta app
2. Request permissions:
   - `instagram_basic` - Basic profile and media access
   - `instagram_content_publish` - Publishing capability
   - `instagram_manage_comments` - Comment management (optional)
3. Submit permission requests with screencasts

### Phase C: Testing (After Approval)
1. Test OAuth flow for Instagram via Meta
2. Test image publishing (Instagram requires image)
3. Test caption with hashtags (max 30)
4. Verify media format requirements (JPEG, min 320x320)

### Technical References
- **Instagram Provider**: `Hazina.Tools.Services.Social\Providers\InstagramProvider.cs`
- **Meta App**: Combined with Facebook task (869c2e17j)
- **OAuth Flow**: Same Meta OAuth infrastructure

### Depends On
- Facebook/Meta API Verification (869c2e17j) - shares same Meta app

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Instagram Business Account active
- [ ] Linked to Facebook Page
- [ ] instagram_content_publish approved
- [ ] OAuth flow tested
- [ ] Image publishing tested
- [ ] Hashtag handling verified

title: TikTok API Verification - Developer Account Registration & App Audit
description:
## SUMMARY (Expert Panel Consensus)
TikTok API requires developer account registration + app audit for Content Posting API access.
Hazina TikTokProvider exists but TikTok has stricter approval process (2-4 weeks typical).
Requires: registered TikTok developer account, app creation, content posting API access request, audit submission.
Lower priority than LinkedIn/Facebook as TikTok content is more video-focused.
Expert panel: TikTok API specialists, short-form video strategists, developer audit consultants.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Phase A: Developer Account (Day 1)
1. Register at developers.tiktok.com
2. Create developer account with business verification
3. Create app: Brand2Boost
4. Configure OAuth redirect URIs

### Phase B: API Products Request (Day 1)
1. Request Content Posting API access
2. Request Login Kit for OAuth
3. Provide:
   - App description and use case
   - Privacy policy URL
   - Terms of service URL
   - Demo video showing intended functionality

### Phase C: Audit Submission (Day 2)
1. Prepare audit materials:
   - Screencast of user flow
   - Description of content types to publish
   - Data handling documentation
   - Security measures description
2. Submit for TikTok audit review
3. Expected timeline: 2-4 weeks

### Phase D: Integration Testing (After Approval)
1. Test OAuth flow with TikTok
2. Test video/image posting
3. Verify content format requirements
4. Test engagement metrics access

### Technical References
- **TikTok Provider**: `Hazina.Tools.Services.Social\Providers\TikTokProvider.cs`
- **OAuth Callback**: `ClientManagerFrontend\src\components\containers\` (TikTok callback)
- **Secrets Config**: `ClientManagerAPI\appsettings.Secrets.json`

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Developer account registered
- [ ] App created and configured
- [ ] Content Posting API approved
- [ ] Login Kit approved
- [ ] OAuth flow tested
- [ ] Content posting tested

title: Medium API Integration - Token Generation & Publishing Setup (Deprecated API Workaround)
description:
## SUMMARY (Expert Panel Consensus)
Medium's official API is deprecated since 2023 - integration requires workaround approach.
Options: (1) Use existing Hazina MediumPublisher with integration token (limited), (2) Browser automation via Playwright.
Integration tokens still work for publishing but no new tokens can be generated via OAuth.
Recommendation: implement with integration token for existing users, plan browser automation as backup.
Expert panel: Medium platform specialists, API deprecation workaround engineers, headless browser automation experts.

---

## Implementation Plan

### Phase A: Assessment (Day 1)
1. Verify current Medium API status:
   - Check if integration tokens still work for `POST /v1/users/{userId}/posts`
   - Test existing Hazina `MediumPublisher` with a test token
   - Document limitations (no image upload, no scheduling, no editing)
2. Decision: proceed with token-based approach or skip Medium

### Phase B: Token-Based Integration (Day 1-2, if viable)
1. Add Medium integration token to user settings:
   - User goes to medium.com/me/settings/security -> Integration tokens
   - User pastes token in Brand2Boost settings
2. Store token securely via existing connected account infrastructure
3. Wire Hazina `MediumPublisher` for publishing
4. Limitations: text + markdown only, no images in API, no editing

### Phase C: Alternative - Browser Automation (Day 2-3, if token deprecated)
1. Use Playwright MCP for browser-based publishing
2. Automate: login -> new story -> paste content -> publish
3. More fragile but handles full feature set

### Technical References
- **Medium Publisher**: `Hazina.Tools.Services.Social\Publishers\MediumPublisher.cs`
- **Medium Provider**: `Hazina.Tools.Services.Social\Providers\MediumProvider.cs`
- **Playwright MCP**: Browser MCP integration

### Definition of Done
- [ ] Medium API viability assessed
- [ ] Integration token flow implemented (if viable)
- [ ] Publishing tested with real Medium account
- [ ] Limitations documented
- [ ] Fallback strategy decided
